title: Crop Rotation Audit for Stable Parcels
summary: Audit French stable agricultural parcels for crop-rotation eligibility and risk, then produce full eligible and flagged GeoPackage outputs with exact schema and layer requirements.
taskPrompt:
You are auditing crop rotation eligibility for stable agricultural parcels in France.

Task directory:
- `base`

Visible inputs:
- Parcel GeoPackage: `base\input\seq1524_d02.gpkg`
- Task brief: `base\input\task_prompt.md`
- Citation note: `base\input\citation.txt`
- Optional Python runtime manifest:
  - `base\input\runtime_env\pyproject.toml`
  - `base\input\runtime_env\uv.lock`
  - `base\input\runtime_env\requirements.txt`
- Software note: `base\software\README.txt`

Optional Python runtime:
- Use `base\software\python_geo.bat` if you want the benchmark-provisioned geospatial Python stack (pinned `numpy`, `pandas`, `geopandas`, `shapely`, `pyogrio`, `fiona`, `pyproj`).
- The package manifest is visible at `base\input\runtime_env\pyproject.toml` if you prefer to build your own environment.

Your task:
1. Read `base\input\task_prompt.md` and follow it exactly.
2. Load the input layer `seq1524_d02` from `base\input\seq1524_d02.gpkg`.
3. Produce these two GeoPackages under `base\output`:
   - `eligible_units.gpkg`
   - `flagged_units.gpkg`
4. Use exact layer names:
   - `eligible_units`
   - `flagged_units`

Rules:
- Do not modify files under `input/`.
- Preserve the input geometry and original columns exactly as required by the task brief.
- Keep the output CRS as EPSG:2154.
- Write final outputs only under `base\output`.
agentMustDo: Read the staged crop-rotation brief in `input/task_prompt.md` and apply it exactly.
Compute the eight required audit columns for every eligible `id_lcp`.
Write `output/eligible_units.gpkg` with preserved original columns and geometry plus the audit fields.
Write `output/flagged_units.gpkg` as the flagged subset with the same schema and exact layer names.

inputFiles.name: seq1524_d02.gpkg
inputFiles.description: Stable agricultural parcel polygons with yearly crop-code columns from 2015 through 2024

inputFiles.name: task_prompt.md
inputFiles.description: Agent-visible crop-rotation audit contract with eligibility and field-computation rules

inputFiles.name: runtime_env/requirements.txt
inputFiles.description: Optional pinned Python geospatial dependencies for an agent-created local runtime

referenceFiles.name: eligible_units.gpkg
referenceFiles.description: Required full eligible-unit output with appended audit fields

referenceFiles.name: flagged_units.gpkg
referenceFiles.description: Required flagged-unit subset output using the same schema and layer ordering

evaluation: Both GeoPackages must exist, be readable, use exact layer names, keep EPSG:2154, contain a unique non-null `id_lcp`, include all eight computed audit fields on the eligible output, match the hidden eligible ID set exactly, and keep `flagged_units.gpkg` on the same schema and column order as `eligible_units.gpkg`. If those gates pass, the normalized score is the raw 100-point scorer divided by 100: 80 points from exact field matches across eligible units and 20 points from flagged count and flagged ID-set correctness.

classifier.rationale: The task involves auditing crop rotation eligibility, which is a key aspect of precision agriculture.
